creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
So this happens with three versions of GCC (4.7, 4.8, 4.9) and three
versions of FreeBSD (9, 10, 11)?

Something must be different in your environment from what basically
everyone else does.  What is that?  Do you have special environment
variables set?  Modifications to files under $PORTS/Mk/*?

Do you have same "unusual" iconv setting or something?

(By the way, even the attached log files carry strange names with
some weird ?UTF-8? characters tossed in.)

In general it's best to report such issues against FreeBSD 10.x or
FreeBSD 9.x at this point.  -CURRENT is, well -CURRENT, and I am
able to spend time looking into the various levels of "we want to
be different" that sometimes occur.
